articles.title: General Motors Just Paid A Record Penalty For Breaking California Privacy Law
articles.summary: General Motors has agreed to pay a record $12.75 million in civil penalties for violating the California Consumer Privacy Act. The company allegedly sold driving data from hundreds of thousands of California motorists to data brokers without their consent, making approximately $20 million from these sales. This settlement is the largest ever for violations of the California Consumer Privacy Act and follows earlier agreements with Honda and Ford for similar breaches.
articles.content: General Motors has agreed to pay a record $12.75 million in civil penalties for violating the California Consumer Privacy Act. The company allegedly sold driving data from hundreds of thousands of California motorists to data brokers without their consent, making approximately $20 million from these sales. This settlement is the largest ever for violations of the California Consumer Privacy Act and follows earlier agreements with Honda and Ford for similar breaches.
